title: 10 best herbal creams for rheumatoid arthritis

remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: These bioactive compounds, particularly curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help reduce joint inflammation and oxidative stress in RA patients.

Cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) inhibition: Turmeric creams containing curcumin have been shown to inhibit COX-2 enzyme activity, thereby reducing the production of prostaglandins and other inflammatory mediators that contribute to RA symptoms.
Anti-cytokine effects: Curcumin in turmeric creams has been found to suppress pro-inflammatory cytokines, such as TNF-alpha and IL-1beta, which play a key role in RA pathology.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, specifically curcumin. Curcumin inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to joint inflammation and damage. By reducing inflammation, curcuma longa creams alleviate symptoms such as pain, stiffness, and swelling in joints affected by rheumatoid arthritis. Additionally, curcuma longa has been shown to modulate the immune system, which may help slow disease progression and improve overall quality of life for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin is a phenolic compound that helps with rheumatoid arthritis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress through its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC is a curcuminoid that helps with rheumatoid arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, while also reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): BDMC is another curcuminoid that helps with rheumatoid arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, while also reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes: Underground stems that store nutrients and are rich in curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots: Contain a high concentration of curcumin and other beneficial compounds that help reduce inflammation and pain.
Seeds: Seeds: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them useful in treating rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes and clean them thoroughly under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Dry the curcuma longa rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 50g of dried curcuma longa rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Combine 10g of the curcuma longa powder with 100g of unsalted butter and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly and heat it to 60c for 10 minutes until the curcuma longa cream thickens and cools.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal ginger creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce inflammation in the joints and alleviate pain associated with rheumatoid arthritis.

Analgesic properties: Ginger's analgesic properties help relieve joint pain and stiffness by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals, such as bradykinin, which can exacerbate rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: Herbal ginger creams have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and tissue degeneration in joints affected by rheumatoid arthritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds present in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to joint inflammation and pain associated with rheumatoid arthritis. By reducing inflammation, ginger creams can help alleviate symptoms and improve quality of life for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis, providing relief from pain and stiffness. This makes ginger creams a promising natural alternative for managing rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols are a class of phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and swelling associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
Shogaols: Shogaols, a type of gingerol, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.

Zingiberene: Zingiberene is a sesquiterpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to protect joints from oxidative damage and reducing inflammation associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots: another part of the plant used, contain a smaller amount of active compounds but are often used in combination with the rhyzome for added benefits.
Leaves: Leaves: are sometimes used to make creams for rheumatoid arthritis, although they contain less active compounds than the rhyzome, their extracts may provide additional health benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 kg of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean and peel the harvested rhizomes then chop them into small pieces weighing approximately 200 grams.
Combine the chopped rhizome pieces with 500 milliliters of a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 40-50 degrees celsius for about 30 minutes stirring occasionally then strain it.
Add 200 milliliters of distilled water to the strained mixture and emulsify it with a natural emulsifier like beeswax and lecithin.

remedy_properties: Salicin: Salicin is a key active compound in white willow bark that helps to reduce pain and inflammation by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and swelling.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: White willow cream contains a range of anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis such as joint pain and stiffness.
Analgesic properties: The salicin and other compounds in white willow bark also have analgesic properties, helping to numb the pain caused by rheumatoid arthritis and provide relief from discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salix alba, also known as white willow, creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The creams contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the joints. Studies have shown that salicin can inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which contribute to the pain and swelling associated with rheumatoid arthritis. By applying the cream topically, salicin can directly target the affected areas, providing relief from pain and inflammation without the need for systemic medications.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: Salicin, a phenolic glycoside, helps alleviate rheumatoid arthritis symptoms by acting as a mild analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, similar to aspirin.
Tannins: Tannins, a type of polyphenol, help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with rheumatoid arthritis, potentially easing joint pain and swelling.

Salicortin: Salicortin, a phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce the severity of rheumatoid arthritis symptoms, such as joint pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Barks: Salix alba barks are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and reduce inflammation associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
Leaves: Salix alba leaves are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation in rheumatoid arthritis.
Stems: Salix alba stems are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of salix alba bark at dawn on a dry day, making sure to identify the plant correctly.
Dry the harvested bark in a single layer at 50°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine the dried bark with 100g of coconut oil and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax is fully incorporated.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and bottle it, allowing it to cool and thicken completely.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Certain herbs like turmeric (curcumin), ginger, and willow bark contain anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce joint swelling and pain in people with RA.

Pain-relieving agents: Herbal creams containing capsaicin (found in chili peppers) or arnica may help alleviate pain by reducing inflammation and blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.
Antioxidant activity: Antioxidant-rich herbs like green tea or chamomile may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with RA, thereby promoting joint health and reducing symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum, also known as bell pepper, creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The capsaicin present in these creams works by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. As a result, the creams reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain, stiffness, and swelling associated with rheumatoid arthritis. This natural remedy has been shown to improve quality of life for patients by reducing reliance on pain medications and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin helps reduce rheumatoid arthritis symptoms by inhibiting the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, thereby providing pain relief.
Quercetin: Quercetin, a type of flavonoid, helps alleviate rheumatoid arthritis symptoms by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and 5-LOX.

β-Carotene: β-Carotene, a precursor to vitamin A, helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with rheumatoid arthritis by scavenging free radicals and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because of their high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce joint pain and inflammation.
Fruits: They are used due to the presence of capsaicin, a natural pain reliever that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.
Seeds: They are used because they contain a high amount of antioxidants and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce joint pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh capsicum annuum fruit in the morning after dew has dried off naturally.
Dry the capsicum annuum fruit at a temperature of 104°f to 115°f for 4-6 hours.
Grind 1/2 cup of dried capsicum annuum fruit into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 1/2 cup of the capsicum annuum powder with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes to 1 hour before straining and cooling it to room temperature.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: A compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, such as tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF-alpha), which are involved in the development of RA.
Flavonoids: These bioactive compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage caused by chronic inflammation, a hallmark of RA.

Triterpenes: St John's Wort triterpenes have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, potentially reducing the severity of joint symptoms and slowing disease progression in RA patients.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because it contains hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help reduce joint inflammation and pain associated with rheumatoid arthritis. Additionally, the cream's ability to increase blood flow and reduce oxidative stress may also contribute to its therapeutic effects. As a result, Hypericum perforatum creams may provide relief from rheumatoid arthritis symptoms, improving quality of life for those affected.
remedy_constituents: Naphthoquinones: These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
Hyperforin: This alkaloid has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help regulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in rheumatoid arthritis.

Flavonoids: These compounds, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate oxidative stress and reduce inflammation in rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.

Flowers: Contain naphthoquinones, specifically hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
Stems: Also contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful in creams for rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 hypericum flowers in mid-summer when they are in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the flowers at 40°c for 2-3 hours or until they reach a crumbly texture.
Grind 20 grams of dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at 60°c.
Combine 5 grams of powdered hypericum with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture stirring constantly.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Purple coneflower creams contain compounds like isoquercitrin and caffeic acid, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating symptoms associated with rheumatoid arthritis such as joint pain and swelling.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in purple coneflower creams, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue destruction characteristic of rheumatoid arthritis.

Immunomodulatory effects: The cream's active compounds, such as luteolin and apigenin, have been found to modulate the immune response, reducing excessive inflammation and immune cell activation that can drive autoimmune diseases like rheumatoid arthritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Echinacea purpurea, such as alkylamides and polyphenols, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain. By reducing inflammation, Echinacea purpurea creams may help slow down the progression of rheumatoid arthritis. Additionally, its antioxidant properties may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, further contributing to its potential benefits in managing rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with rheumatoid arthritis.

Caffeic acid phenethyl ester (CAPE): As a phenolic compound, CAPE exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis such as joint pain and inflammation.

Cichoric acid: This phenolic compound has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in the joints of individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.

Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea are used for their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with rheumatoid arthritis.

Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea are used for their high content of caffeic acid and other phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis, such as pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers in the late morning or early afternoon when the dew has evaporated.
Cleanse 1/4 cup of the harvested flowers with 1 tablespoon of witch hazel in a small bowl to remove impurities.
Steep 1/4 cup of the cleaned flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to create a floral infusion.
Strain the floral infusion through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan and heat until the beeswax melts.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Devil's Claw contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in joints, alleviating symptoms of RA.
Analgesic and anesthetic effects: The cream's active ingredients can help numb the affected area, providing relief from chronic pain and discomfort associated with RA.
Immunomodulatory properties: Devil's Claw has been shown to modulate the immune system, which may help reduce inflammation and joint damage caused by RA.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Harpagophytum procumbens, also known as devil's claw, creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because it contains harpogoside, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces joint pain and swelling. The cream's active ingredients penetrate deep into the skin, targeting the affected areas and providing quick relief from discomfort. Additionally, devil's claw has been shown to improve joint mobility and reduce morning stiffness, allowing individuals with rheumatoid arthritis to manage their symptoms and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Harpagoside: This iridoid glycoside acts as a potent anti-inflammatory agent, reducing joint inflammation and pain associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
Procumbideside: Similar to harpagoside, procumbideside has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate the symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis, including joint pain and swelling.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes, such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, found in Harpagophytum procumbens, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in joints affected by rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Harpagophytum procumbens are primarily used due to their high content of harpagoside, a key compound responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, which are underground stems that store nutrients, are used for their concentrated amounts of harpagoside and other bioactive compounds.
Stems: The stems are utilized for their secondary metabolites, which contribute to the overall anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects of the plant-based creams for rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried harpagophytum procumbens root and place it in a clean glass jar.
Mix 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil with 20 grams of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax melts.
Add the dried harpagophytum procumbens root to the mixture and let it steep for 2 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ashwagandha cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with RA, alleviating symptoms such as joint stiffness and tenderness.

Immune-modulatory: Ashwagandha's immune-regulating properties can help modulate the immune system to prevent excessive inflammation and tissue damage caused by RA, thereby reducing disease progression.
Analgesic: The cream's analgesic properties provide relief from pain associated with RA, allowing for improved quality of life and reduced reliance on pain medications.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera, also known as ashwagandha, creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal extract reduces joint inflammation and pain associated with rheumatoid arthritis. It also helps to reduce oxidative stress, which contributes to the disease's progression. Additionally, ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties may help alleviate stress and anxiety, common comorbidities of rheumatoid arthritis, thereby improving overall quality of life. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative to conventional treatments for managing rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroidal lactones have potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with rheumatoid arthritis.

Withaferin A: This withanolide has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, such as TNF-alpha and IL-1beta, which play a key role in the pathogenesis of rheumatoid arthritis.

Ashwagandhans A and B: These withanolides have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in joints affected by rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in withanolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce joint inflammation and pain.
Leaves: Contain withanolides and other bioactive compounds that help in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in joints affected by rheumatoid arthritis.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and withanolides, which help in reducing inflammation and promoting overall health and well-being in individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Extract 30-50 grams of dried withania somnifera root from a reputable supplier.
Grind the extracted root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Combine the powder with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids immediately.
Whip the mixture with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter using a hand mixer for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salannin, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with Rheumatoid Arthritis.
Antioxidant activity: The creams' antioxidant properties help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of Rheumatoid Arthritis.
Analgesic and anesthetic effects: The creams' analgesic and anesthetic properties help numb pain and reduce discomfort, making it easier for people with Rheumatoid Arthritis to manage their symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the joints, thereby alleviating symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis such as pain and stiffness. The creams also improve blood flow to the affected areas, promoting healing and reducing the severity of joint inflammation. This leads to improved mobility and reduced discomfort for individuals suffering from this condition.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This terpene helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to the development and progression of rheumatoid arthritis.

Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): These phenolic compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis, such as joint pain and swelling.

Ginkgotoxin (Ginkgolide B): This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system's response to inflammation and reduce joint damage associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are used to make creams for rheumatoid arthritis because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are used to make creams for rheumatoid arthritis because they contain bilobalide and ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba are used to make creams for rheumatoid arthritis because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils in a pot of boiling water for 15 minutes to prevent contamination.
Combine 30 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 500 milliliters of jojoba oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours to allow the ginkgo to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids.
Whip 50 grams of beeswax and 200 grams of shea butter into the infused oil mixture until it thickens.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling, pain, and stiffness associated with RA by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.
Antioxidant: Frankincense's antioxidant properties protect joints from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue degeneration in RA.
Anti-rheumatic: Frankincense has been shown to inhibit the activity of enzymes that break down joint cartilage and bone, thereby slowing down the progression of joint destruction in RA.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata, also known as frankincense, creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the joints. The active compound, boswellic acid, blocks the production of inflammatory chemicals, such as leukotrienes, that cause joint damage. This leads to relief from morning stiffness, reduced joint pain, and improved mobility. Additionally, Boswellia serrata creams may also help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, promoting overall joint health and reducing the progression of rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Alpha-boswellic acids: These terpenoid compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help reduce inflammation and joint pain in rheumatoid arthritis patients.

Boswellic acids: Similar to alpha-boswellic acids, boswellic acids also exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties, thereby alleviating symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis such as swelling and stiffness.

Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): This specific boswellic acid has potent anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic effects, which help reduce joint inflammation and pain in rheumatoid arthritis patients.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Boswellia serrata contain boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, making them suitable for creams used to treat rheumatoid arthritis.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Boswellia serrata are also a source of boswellic acids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis, making them a preferred part for creams.
remedy_recipe: Extract 2 teaspoons of boswellia serrata resin from capsules or raw material and set aside for later use.
Combine 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler to melt slowly.
Stir in 10 drops of vitamin e oil and 10 drops of essential oil into the melted mixture carefully.
Add the boswellia serrata resin to the mixture and stir until well combined and smooth texture forms slowly.
Pour the mixture into a container and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to set and thicken completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the joints and surrounding tissues, alleviating symptoms such as pain, stiffness, and swelling. Additionally, licorice cream's flavonoids and saponins have potent antioxidant effects, which help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by chronic inflammation. By reducing inflammation and promoting joint health, licorice creams may provide relief for individuals suffering from rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage joints and contribute to inflammation, potentially slowing down the progression of rheumatoid arthritis.
Liquiritigenin: This isoflavanoid has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive effects, which can help reduce joint inflammation and alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps reduce joint pain and swelling.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and triterpenes that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in relieving arthritic symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 200ml of distilled water in a saucepan over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Combine the infused liquid with 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler on medium heat for 10 minutes.
Add 100ml of aloe vera gel and 5% w/w vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature below 40°f for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol present in peppermint relaxes muscles, reduces pain, and soothes stiffness in joints, providing relief from symptoms of the condition. Additionally, peppermint's cooling effect can help reduce inflammation by constricting blood vessels and reducing swelling. This topical application allows for targeted relief directly at the site of discomfort, making it a natural and effective way to manage rheumatoid arthritis symptoms without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal peppermint creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol, menthone, and limonene, which can help reduce inflammation in the joints, a key symptom of RA.
Pain-relieving properties: Peppermint's analgesic properties may help alleviate joint pain, stiffness, and soreness associated with RA by blocking pain signals to the brain.

Antioxidant and free radical scavenging activities: Peppermint's antioxidant properties can help protect joints from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue damage in RA.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
Leaves: High in menthol and menthone, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can provide relief from joint pain and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of dried peppermint leaves and 100ml of distilled water for infusion.
Steep the dried peppermint leaves in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create an herbal tea.
Strain the peppermint tea through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 25g of coconut oil with 20ml of the peppermint tea, stirring until smooth.
Pour the mixture into sterilized cream tubes and store at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb calends, extracted from the flower's petals, has been traditionally used to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in joints affected by the condition. When applied topically as a cream, calends works to soothe inflamed tissues, reducing stiffness and improving joint mobility. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate arthritis symptoms, leading to improved overall comfort and reduced discomfort associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce swelling and pain in joints affected by RA.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold cream help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate RA symptoms.

Wound-healing and anti-scarring properties: Pot marigold's triterpenoids and flavonoids promote wound healing and reduce scarring, making it helpful for treating skin lesions and ulcers commonly associated with RA.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent in anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots are valued for their ability to soothe joint pain and reduce inflammation associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
Barks: The barks contain salicylic acid, a natural derivative of aspirin, which helps to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and prevent further tissue damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers weighing 20 grams and grind them into fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the ground calendula powder with 100 milliliters of jojoba oil in a glass bottle and infuse for 2 hours at room temperature.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the solids from the liquid, discarding the solids after 15 minutes.
Mix 30 grams of beeswax pellets with 50 milliliters of the calendula-infused oil in a double boiler and melt over low heat for 10 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and stir until the mixture cools to around 40°c, then whip the cream using an electric mixer for 2 minutes until it becomes smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce swelling and pain in joints affected by RA. Additionally, the calming effects of English lavender can help alleviate stress and anxiety often associated with chronic conditions like RA. By soothing and relaxing the mind and body, herbal English lavender creams can provide much-needed relief from symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis, allowing individuals to better manage their condition and improve overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lavender's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce joint pain and swelling by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation in the affected joints.

Cicatrizant and Analgesic properties: English lavender's cicatrizant (wound-healing) and analgesic (pain-relieving) properties can help to reduce pain, stiffness, and swelling associated with Rheumatoid Arthritis by promoting healing and reducing discomfort in the affected joints.

Sedative and Calming effects: Lavender's calming and sedative properties can help to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, which are common comorbidities with Rheumatoid Arthritis, and improve overall sleep quality, all of which can contribute to improved joint health and reduced symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with rheumatoid arthritis.

Flowers: Lavender flowers are rich in essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective for topical creams to reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered lavender with 100g of beeswax, 50g of coconut oil, and 20g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 10ml of jojoba oil and stir well to combine, then continue heating for an additional 15 minutes or until the mixture reaches 60°c to 65°c.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool slightly before whipping the mixture with a hand whisk for 5 minutes until it thickens into a creamy consistency.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars, label them with the date and contents, and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature of 4°c to 6°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its unique blend of anti-inflammatory compounds, particularly eucalyptol and cineole. These active ingredients work synergistically to reduce inflammation and relieve joint pain and stiffness associated with the condition. The topical application of the cream allows for direct delivery of these compounds to the affected areas, providing targeted relief without systemic absorption. As a result, Tasmanian blue gum creams offer a natural and effective solution for managing rheumatoid arthritis symptoms, promoting improved mobility and quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains bioactive compounds like eucalyptol and globulol, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, reducing joint swelling and pain associated with RA.

Antioxidant properties: Eucalyptus globulus has high antioxidant activity, which helps neutralize free radicals that contribute to joint damage and inflammation in RA patients, thereby protecting against oxidative stress.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: The essential oils present in Tasmanian Blue Gum creams have been shown to exhibit pain-relieving and analgesic effects, providing relief from chronic joint pain and stiffness characteristic of RA.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to reduce joint pain and swelling associated with Rheumatoid Arthritis.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, barks have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, such as inflammation and joint stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of high-quality eucalyptus globulus essential oil, 400ml of carrier oil and filter them for quality control purposes.
Heat 300ml of beeswax in a double boiler until it reaches its melting point of 64-66°c to create the base mixture.
Slowly pour 20 drops of eucalyptus globulus essential oil into the melted beeswax, stirring thoroughly to ensure uniform distribution.
Add 200ml of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, to the mixture and stir until it reaches a consistent temperature around 40-45°c.
Pour the final mixture into sterilized containers and let it cool at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that target the root cause of the condition. These acids work to reduce inflammation and pain in the joints, providing relief for sufferers of rheumatoid arthritis. Additionally, frankincense's ability to stimulate blood flow and reduce stiffness makes it an effective natural remedy for improving joint mobility and reducing morning stiffness associated with this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Frankincense contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds like boswellic acids, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reduce inflammation in joints, alleviating RA symptoms.

Pain relief: The sesquiterpenoids present in frankincense have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, helping to reduce pain, stiffness, and swelling associated with RA, allowing for improved mobility and quality of life.
Antioxidant activity: Frankincense's antioxidant properties help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the progression of RA and preserving joint health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.
Barks: Contain oleoresins, which possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities, making them effective in reducing joint pain and inflammation associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried boswellia frereana resin and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle within 2 hours.
Combine 200 milliliters of sweet almond oil with the powdered boswellia frereana in a double boiler and heat for 1 hour at 60 degrees celsius.
Add 10 milliliters of beeswax and stir until melted, then remove from heat and let cool for 30 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of shea butter with 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil and add to the cooled mixture, stirring well within 15 minutes.
Pour the cream into a sterilized jar and label, storing in a dark place at room temperature for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its unique properties that target the root causes of inflammation and pain. The cream's active compounds, including cinnamaldehyde and cinnamic acid, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to reduce joint swelling and stiffness. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby decreasing the body's natural response to inflammation. This combination of actions makes Ceylon cinnamon creams a promising natural remedy for alleviating symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_properties: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and pain in joints affected by RA.
Methyl Hydroxycinnamate (MHIC): MHIC has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing joint inflammation and damage caused by RA.

Trans-Cinnamic Acid: This compound has been found to have anti-arthritic properties, including the ability to reduce joint swelling and inflammation, as well as inhibiting the activity of enzymes that break down cartilage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing joint inflammation and pain associated with Rheumatoid Arthritis.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of cinnamaldehyde, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of Rheumatoid Arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinnamomum verum bark and dry it in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Grind dried cinnamomum verum into a fine powder using a spice grinder and sift through a 100-mesh sieve.
Mix 2 tablespoons of cinnamomum verum powder with 1 tablespoon of beeswax pellets and 6 tablespoons of coconut oil in a heatproof bowl.
Place the bowl in a double boiler and warm over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches 140°f to 150°f.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool to around 100°f before transferring the cream to a clean glass jar and labeling for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because it contains bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory properties. The sulfur-containing compounds in garlic, such as allicin, work to reduce inflammation and relieve joint pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, garlic's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage joints and contribute to disease progression. By targeting both inflammation and oxidative stress, herbal garlic creams may provide a natural and effective solution for managing rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Allicin: Allicin, a sulfur compound found in garlic, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with RA.
S-Allyl Cysteine (SAC): SAC, another compound found in garlic, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, reducing inflammation and joint damage caused by RA.
Methyl Sulfanyl Methane (MSM): MSM, a naturally occurring sulfur compound, can help reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility by increasing collagen synthesis and reducing cartilage degradation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, garlic roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The underground stem of garlic plants contains sulfur-containing compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making it effective in reducing pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh garlic (allium sativum) bulbs and peel them carefully.
Dry the peeled garlic in a cool dark place for 7-10 days or using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 12 hours.
Grind the dried garlic into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle and mix with 100g of sweet almond oil and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture slowly over low heat while stirring occasionally until the beeswax has melted completely and the mixture reaches a temperature of 75°c to 80°c for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat and let the mixture cool slightly before whipping it into a smooth cream using an electric mixer or whisk for about 2-3 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because they contain the active compound parthenolide, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. This natural ingredient is able to reduce inflammation in the joints, providing relief from pain and stiffness associated with rheumatoid arthritis. Additionally, feverfew's ability to relax muscle tissue can help alleviate joint tension and discomfort, allowing for improved mobility and reduced morning stiffness. Overall, feverfew creams offer a natural alternative to traditional medications for managing symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like parthenolide, which help reduce inflammation in the joints, a hallmark symptom of RA.
Analgesic and anxiolytic effects: The active ingredients in feverfew cream, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, have been shown to provide relief from pain and anxiety associated with RA.

Antioxidant properties: Feverfew's antioxidant properties help protect the body against oxidative stress caused by chronic inflammation, which can damage joint tissues and contribute to RA progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing joint pain and inflammation.
Flowers: Containing a high concentration of parthenolide, a potent bioactive compound that has been shown to reduce prostaglandin synthesis, leading to decreased inflammation and pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Collect dried tanacetum parthenium leaves of high quality and store them in an airtight container.
Measure out 50 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium leaves and grind them into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the tanacetum parthenium powder with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 180°f on a thermometer.
Pour the warm mixture into glass jars and let it cool and solidify completely before using as needed to alleviate rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for rheumatoid arthritis are topical formulations that contain plant extracts to help alleviate symptoms of the condition. They work by reducing inflammation and pain, providing relief from joint stiffness and swelling. The best herbal creams for rheumatoid arthritis include Curcuma longa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Aloe barbadensis, Arnica montana, Capsicum annuum, Equisetum arvense, Camellia sinensis, Salix alba, and Boswellia serrata. If left untreated, rheumatoid arthritis can severely impact daily life, causing chronic pain, limited mobility, and emotional distress, ultimately affecting work, relationships, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to rheumatoid arthritis that are treated with herbal creams are conditions like osteoarthritis, psoriatic arthritis, and fibromyalgia. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as capsaicin, menthol, and arnica gel provide topical relief for joint pain, stiffness, and inflammation. Additionally, creams infused with essential oils like lavender, peppermint, and ginger may help alleviate symptoms of chronic pain, fatigue, and mood disturbances associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with rheumatoid arthritis is often a blend of arnica, capsaicin, and frankincense. Arnica cream, derived from the arnica flower, is known to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. Capsaicin cream, made from the chili pepper, stimulates blood flow and blocks pain signals. Meanwhile, frankincense cream, extracted from the boswellia tree, reduces inflammation and promotes healing. A combination of these creams may provide relief from rheumatoid arthritis symptoms, such as pain and stiffness, and may even help to slow disease progression.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which target the root causes of the condition. Curcuma longa creams also possess antioxidant properties, which protect the joints from oxidative stress and promote healing. As a result, patients experience improved mobility and reduced symptoms, making glycyrrhiza glabra creams a valuable natural remedy for managing rheumatoid arthritis.

Zingiber officinale creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, ginger has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which are molecules that promote inflammation. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Zingiber officinale creams may help to alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis and improve quality of life.

Aloe barbadensis creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the joints. The cream's moisturizing properties help to reduce inflammation and pain by hydrating the skin and relieving dryness. Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with rheumatoid arthritis, promoting joint health and reducing symptoms. Regular use of aloe vera creams may help alleviate joint pain and discomfort.

Arnica montana creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and pain in the affected joints. The gel or cream form allows for easy topical application, directly targeting the inflamed areas and reducing discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of Arnica montana also help to reduce stiffness and improve joint mobility, providing relief from the chronic pain and swelling associated with rheumatoid arthritis, allowing patients to regain their quality of life. Regular use may also aid in managing symptoms.

Capsicum annuum creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsaicin in these creams binds to pain receptors, reducing the transmission of pain signals to the brain. By desensitizing these receptors, capsaicin cream reduces pain and inflammation associated with rheumatoid arthritis. Additionally, capsaicin has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, such as bradykinin, which contribute to the inflammation and pain experienced by individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. This makes Capsicum annuum creams a popular natural remedy for managing rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.

Equisetum arvense creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce joint pain and swelling. The herb, also known as Horsetail, contains flavonoids and saponins that help to relax muscles and calm inflammation. By applying Equisetum arvense creams topically, the active ingredients are absorbed directly into the skin, where they can target the affected joints and provide relief from symptoms such as stiffness and soreness, allowing for improved mobility and quality of life.

Camellia sinensis creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream is derived from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant, the same plant used to make green tea. The active compounds in the cream, such as catechins and theaflavins, have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. By applying the cream topically, it can help to soothe and calm inflamed joints, reducing pain and stiffness associated with rheumatoid arthritis. This natural approach may provide relief without harsh chemicals.

Salix alba creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active compounds, such as salicylic acid, help reduce swelling and ease pain in affected joints. Additionally, salix alba's anti-oxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress, which can contribute to joint damage. By applying the cream topically, salix alba creams can provide targeted relief from rheumatoid arthritis symptoms, promoting a reduction in pain and inflammation. Boswellia serrata creams helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The boswellic acids present in the herb reduce inflammation and pain in the joints, providing relief from symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis. By reducing inflammation and pain, Boswellia serrata creams enable individuals to maintain their mobility and flexibility, improving their overall quality of life.
